action: Click on the Windows Start button (bottom left) to open the Start menu.
fallback_action: Perform a Google search using Internet Explorer

recipe.steps:
is the first action. The instruction is to click on the Windows Start button at the bottom left to open the Start menu. Based on the detected bounding boxes, Box ID 35 corresponds to the Start button.
Action Description: Click on the Windows Start button to open the Start menu.

previous action: The instruction was to click on the Windows Start button (Box ID 35) to open the Start menu. On the current screen, the Start menu is now visible, confirming the previous action succeeded. The next step would typically be to search for or select an application, such as Notepad, from the Start menu. To proceed, I will type 'Notepad' to search for the application.
Action Description: Type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar to search for the Notepad application.

previous action: The instruction was to type 'Notepad' in the Start menu search bar. On the current screen, the Start menu is open and the word 'Notepad' is visible in the search area, confirming the previous action succeeded. The next step is to launch Notepad. Typically, after searching, the Notepad app icon appears in the results and should be clicked to open the application. I will now left-click on the Notepad result to launch it.
Action Description: Click on the Notepad application in the Start menu search results to open Notepad.

previous action: The instruction was to click on the Notepad application in the Start menu search results. On the current screen, the Start menu is no longer visible and there is no other application window open in the foreground, which suggests Notepad may have launched but is not yet visible, or it may be loading. There is no clear indication of Notepad's window or text area yet. To account for possible loading time, I will wait for 1 second to allow Notepad to open.
Action Description: Wait for 1 second to allow Notepad to finish opening.

previous action: I waited 1 second to allow Notepad to open. On the current screen, the Notepad window is now visible in the foreground. The window is mostly empty, indicating the main text area is ready for input. This confirms that Notepad has successfully launched and is ready for typing. The next step is to type the desired text into Notepad. Since the main text area is visually identifiable as the large empty region in the center of the Notepad window, I will click in the center of this area to ensure focus before typing.
Action Description: Click in the center of the Notepad text area to ensure it is focused for typing.

previous action: I clicked in the center of the Notepad text area to ensure it is focused for typing. The current screen shows the Notepad window in the foreground, and the main text area appears ready for input. There are no other overlays or dialogs, and the area is empty, indicating it is ready to receive text. The next step is to type the desired text into Notepad.
Action Description: Type the desired text into the Notepad text area.
Type 'Hello, this is a test of Notepad automation.'
